quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, was named after a Greek navigator who sailed for Spain in 1592. His real name was Ioannis Phokas, but the Spanish called him Juan de Fuca. He claimed to have discovered the passage between the Pacific Ocean and the inland waters, though historians still debate whether he actually made the voyage!

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals, those adorable spotted friends you might spot lounging on rocks near Port Angeles, can hold their breath for nearly 30 minutes and dive over 1,400 feet deep! Learn about their fascinating underwater world and family life.

medium_read.title: The Surprising History of Tulips and Hungary
medium_read.teaser: Tulips aren't just a Dutch treasure—they have deep roots in Hungarian culture and gardens too! This delightful article explores how these beloved spring flowers traveled from the Ottoman Empire to become symbols of beauty across Europe.

long_read.title: Olympic National Park: A Wilderness Crown
long_read.teaser: From glaciated mountain peaks to temperate rainforests to wild Pacific coastline, Olympic National Park contains an extraordinary diversity of ecosystems right in your backyard. This comprehensive guide celebrates the natural wonders that make this place so magical.

port_angeles.title: Bald Eagles Along the Strait
port_angeles.description: Late May is a wonderful time to spot bald eagles along the Strait of Juan de Fuca! These magnificent birds nest in the tall trees near the waterfront and can often be seen fishing or soaring on thermal currents. Port Angeles is home to one of the healthiest bald eagle populations in the lower 48 states, with dozens of nesting pairs in the area.

puzzle_1.question: A bald eagle's nest, called an aerie, can weigh up to ___ pounds when fully built over many years.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That's about as much as a small car—incredible!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! These massive nests can actually reach 2,000 pounds.
puzzle_1.wrong_response: These amazing nests can weigh up to 2,000 pounds—as heavy as a small car! Eagles add to them year after year.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Bald eagles are faithful to their nesting sites and return to the same nest year after year, adding new sticks and materials each season. The largest bald eagle nest on record was over 9 feet wide and 20 feet deep!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Beautiful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly—such a lovely, delicate word!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound as delicate as the creature itself?
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language, part of the Finno-Ugric family, and is not related to the surrounding Slavic or Germanic languages. It's known for its beautiful, poetic words and complex but logical grammar.

puzzle_3.question: What type of forest covers much of the western slopes of Olympic National Park?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! The temperate rainforests here are among the wettest places in the continental US, receiving over 12 feet of rain annually!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's actually temperate rainforest! These lush, moss-draped forests receive over 12 feet of rain each year and are some of the most beautiful in the world.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Olympic National Park's Hoh Rainforest is one of the finest examples of temperate rainforest in the United States, with Sitka spruce and western hemlock trees reaching over 300 feet tall and draped in club moss and ferns.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
